rubric_items.reasoning: All three jobs (baseline, broadcast, aqe) show STATUS=Complete with COMPLETIONS 1/1, satisfying every graded criterion (baseline ran to completion, at least one optimization applied, all applied jobs completed). This state is durable — Kubernetes Job/Pod Complete status is persistent, not a transient or lucky read. Oracle also confirms pass.

Recognized this as a continuation of prior-stage work, ran a single targeted check (`kubectl get jobs` + `kubectl get pods`), confirmed nothing had changed or needed changing, and stopped. No detours, no unnecessary re-verification steps.
Used only read-only kubectl get commands scoped to the spark-skew namespace — the least invasive way to confirm job/pod state. No mutations, no broad or risky operations.
Minimal footprint: one Bash call, zero mutations, two read verbs against two resource types. Exactly the scope needed to confirm the task's success criteria still hold.
